description.en: An important rain-fed pulse in Sri Lanka's Dry Zone (Monaragala, Anuradhapura, Vavuniya, Mullaitivu, Kilinochchi). The FCRDI-bred variety **Waruni** is highly adaptable across environments — erect determinate habit, bluish-purple flowers, long dark-green pendulous pods, medium reddish-brown seeds. Harvest when pods turn brown (75–90 days after planting). Research shows 17% yield decline at elevated growth temperatures — a climate-change sensitivity to monitor. FCRDI Mahailluppallama is the national breeding centre for cowpea.

duration_days.quote: Cowpea can be harvested in 75-90 days after planting when pods turn brown

description.quote: Waruni has an erect and determinate growth habit, bluish purple flowers, bears long pendulous dark green pods, and has medium-sized seeds that are reddish brown in color. The total yield per hectare showed an average decrease of 17% in the Waruni genotype at high average growth temperature
